description:
The gnutls-utils package can be installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf install gnutls-utils
rationale:
GnuTLS is a secure communications library implementing the SSL, TLS and DTLS
protocols and technologies around them. It provides a simple C language
application programming interface (API) to access the secure communications
protocols as well as APIs to parse and write X.509, PKCS #12, OpenPGP and
other required structures.
This package contains command line TLS client and server and certificate
manipulation tools.
ocil_clause: the gnutls-utils package is not installed
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the gnutls-utils package is installed: 
$ rpm -q gnutls-utils
fixtext:
To install the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 package gnutls-utils run the following command:

$ sudo dnf install gnutls-utils
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the gnutls-utils package installed.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the gnutls-utils package installed.
stig.vuldiscussion: GnuTLS is a secure communications library implementing the SSL, TLS and DTLS protocols and technologies around them. It provides a simple C language application programming interface (API) to access the secure communications protocols as well as APIs to parse and write X.509, PKCS #12, OpenPGP and other required structures. This package contains command line TLS client and server and certificate manipulation tools.
stig.checktext:
Verify that Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 has the gnutls-utils package installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed gnutls-utils

Example output:

gnutls-utils.x86_64          3.7.3-9.el9

If the "gnutls-utils" package is not installed, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
The gnutls-utils package can be installed with the following command:

$ sudo dnf install gnutls-utils

title: Ensure gnutls-utils is installed
